topics.name: 100–200 Level: Circuit Theory, Electronics, and Engineering Mathematics
topics.description: EEE 101 Circuit Theory, EEE 201 Electronics I, MTH 211 Engineering Mathematics.

subtopics.name: EEE 101: DC and AC Circuit Analysis at FUTO
objectives: Apply Kirchhoff's laws, Thevenin's and Norton's theorems to analyse electrical circuits
Solve AC circuits using phasors, admittance, and power factor correction methods

subtopics.name: EEE 201: Analog and Digital Electronics
objectives: Explain operation of diodes, BJTs, FETs, and op-amps in analog circuits
Design combinational and sequential logic circuits using standard IC families

topics.name: 300 Level: Power Systems, Electromagnetic Theory, and Instrumentation
topics.description: EEE 301 Power Systems, EEE 311 Electromagnetic Fields, EEE 321 Electrical Measurements.

subtopics.name: EEE 301: Power Systems Analysis at FUTO
objectives: Model single-phase and three-phase power systems including transformers and generators
Perform load flow analysis and fault level calculations for Nigerian power network scenarios

subtopics.name: EEE 321: Electronic Instrumentation and Measurements
objectives: Calibrate and operate oscilloscopes, multimeters, function generators, and spectrum analysers
Apply signal conditioning, transducer interfacing, and data acquisition to industrial measurement

topics.name: 400–500 Level: Power Electronics, Renewable Energy, Control & Project
topics.description: EEE 401 Power Electronics, EEE 411 Renewable Energy Systems, EEE 499 Final Year Project.

subtopics.name: EEE 401 & EEE 411: Power Electronics and Solar PV Systems
objectives: Design DC-DC converters, inverters, and rectifiers for industrial power conversion
Size and design solar PV, wind, and mini-hydro systems for off-grid Nigerian communities

subtopics.name: EEE 499: FUTO Electrical Engineering Final Year Project
objectives: Design, build, and test an electrical engineering solution addressing an industrial or community need
Document and defend project with technical report before FUTO School of Engineering board
